seoTitle: How to Record Credit Card Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record and categorize credit card transactions in QuickBooks Online so your expenses stay organized and reconciliation stays simple.
intro: Business credit card activity needs to be entered and categorized consistently to keep your expense reporting accurate. This guide walks through recording credit card transactions in QuickBooks Online from entry to reconciliation.

steps.heading: Connect or set up the credit card account
steps.body: Make sure the credit card account is set up in QuickBooks Online, ideally connected to a live bank feed.

steps.heading: Review incoming transactions
steps.body: Go through the transactions pulled in from the feed, or enter them manually if you're not using a live connection.

steps.heading: Categorize each transaction
steps.body: Assign each transaction to the correct expense category so your reports reflect accurate spending.

steps.heading: Reconcile the account
steps.body: Once your statement arrives, reconcile the account to confirm every transaction is accounted for.

faq.question: Should I categorize transactions before or after reconciling?
faq.answer: It's best to categorize transactions as they come in, then reconcile once your statement is available to confirm accuracy.

faq.question: What if a transaction shows up twice?
faq.answer: Check for duplicate entries, especially if you're both manually entering and pulling in a bank feed, and exclude or delete the duplicate.

faq.question: Can I split a credit card transaction across categories?
faq.answer: Yes, you can split a single transaction into multiple categories if it covers more than one type of expense.

faq.question: How do I handle credit card payments themselves?
faq.answer: A payment toward your credit card balance is typically recorded as a transfer from your bank account to the credit card account, not as an expense.
